Life360 Shs Chess Depository Interests Repr 3 Sh ( (AU:360) ) has provided an announcement.

Life360 will release its first quarter 2026 results for the period ended March 31, 2026 to the Australian Securities Exchange on May 12, 2026 AEST, with a corresponding investor conference call hosted by CEO Lauren Antonoff and CFO Russell Burke. The Zoom audio webinar, which will include a Q&A session for registered participants and a replay available on the company’s investor site, underscores Life360’s ongoing engagement with investors as it reports on performance in its rapidly scaling global family safety platform business.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:360) stock is a Buy with a A$35.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Life360 Shs Chess Depository Interests Repr 3 Sh stock, see the AU:360 Stock Forecast page.

More about Life360 Shs Chess Depository Interests Repr 3 Sh

Life360, Inc. is a family connection and safety company that offers a category-leading mobile app and Tile tracking devices designed to keep people close to the ones they love. Its services include location sharing, safe driver reports, and crash detection with emergency dispatch, serving about 95.8 million monthly active users across more than 180 countries as of December 31, 2025.

Simble Solutions Ltd. ( (AU:SIS) ) has issued an update.

Simble Solutions has disclosed a change in the shareholding of director Stephen Thornhill following an issue of securities approved by shareholders. Thornhill acquired 4,000,000 ordinary shares at $0.005 each and 1,333,334 unlisted options exercisable at $0.01, which mature in April 2029.

Following the transaction, Thornhill’s holding increased to 19,031,111 ordinary shares and a total of 11,333,334 unlisted options across two series. The new securities were issued on the same terms as those offered to unrelated placement participants, indicating alignment between director and broader investor pricing in the recent capital raising.

More about Simble Solutions Ltd.

Simble Solutions Limited is an Australia-based company listed on the ASX. The firm operates in the technology and software sector, though this notice focuses specifically on director shareholdings rather than detailing its product suite or customer markets.

An announcement from Hot Chili Limited ( (AU:HCH) ) is now available.

Hot Chili director Roberto de Andraca Adriasola has increased his direct holding of the company’s U.S. over-the-counter securities, purchasing 50,000 additional HHLKF shares on 9 April 2026 at USD 1.038 each. Following the on-market trade, his interests now total 100,000 ASX-listed shares, 100,000 OTC shares and 87,000 service rights, signalling strengthened director alignment with shareholders but no change in contractual interests.

The transaction was conducted as an on-market purchase on the OTCQX platform and did not occur during a closed period requiring special clearance. data.title: Critica Boosts Rare Earth Recoveries as Jupiter Pilot Plant Optimisation Advances
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Venture Minerals Limited ( (AU:CRI) ).

Critica Limited has reported significant progress at its Jupiter Rare Earth Project, with pilot plant optimisation lifting total rare earth oxide recovery to about 71% and magnetic rare earth recovery to roughly 81% from representative feed material. The enhanced beneficiation circuit delivers a roughly 7.5-times upgrade from feed to intermediate concentrate and rejects about 90% of mass, providing stronger technical support for the project’s development assumptions.

The pilot plant has been relocated to a larger facility to increase throughput and support further optimisation, with over 8.5 tonnes of additional mineralisation en route and more to follow for concentrate production. Parallel metallurgical programmes, including work on acid bake, alternative leaching and potential heap leaching of intermediate concentrate, are underway to refine processing pathways and inform a Scoping Study that remains on schedule, underpinning future product qualification and offtake discussions.

More about Venture Minerals Limited

Critica Limited is an ASX-listed resources company focused on developing the Jupiter Rare Earth Project, targeting high-value magnetic rare earth elements such as neodymium and praseodymium. The company is advancing beneficiation and downstream processing technologies to produce mixed rare earth products for use in advanced manufacturing and clean energy applications.

Average Trading Volume: 7,948,636

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: A$72.74M

data.title: Marti Technologies’ Earnings Call Signals Profitable Pivot
data.description:
Marti Technologies Inc ((MRT)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Marti Technologies’ latest earnings call struck an optimistic tone as management highlighted a sharp operational inflection. Revenue more than doubled, margins swung strongly into positive territory, and engagement indicators surged, giving credibility to ambitious 2026 profitability targets despite ongoing losses and funding sensitivities.

Revenue Surge Builds Confidence in Scale

Total revenue jumped 110% year over year to $39.2 million, beating guidance by $5.2 million and underscoring rapid platform scaling. The outperformance reinforces management’s confidence in reaching roughly $70 million of revenue in 2026 as monetization spreads beyond the limited 2025 city footprint.

Gross Margin Swings From Red to Robust

Marti delivered a dramatic gross margin turnaround, moving from a 15.5% loss in 2024 to a 61.1% margin in 2025 and lifting gross profit by $26.9 million. Management attributed the shift to platform monetization, operating leverage and efficiency initiatives, signaling a structurally stronger business model.

Adjusted EBITDA Loss Narrows but Remains a Drag

Adjusted EBITDA loss improved by 37% to negative $12.1 million, beating guidance by $4.9 million and showcasing better cost control and monetization. Still, the company remains in the red and is aiming for a $1 million positive adjusted EBITDA in 2026, implying a sizable $13.1 million further turnaround.

User Growth and Engagement Accelerate

Platform activity expanded rapidly, with total trips up 60% to 50.8 million and unique consumers rising 44% to 3.1 million. Trips per user climbed 11% to 16.5, indicating stronger engagement and deeper cross-service usage across Marti’s mobility offerings.

Ride-Hailing Network Scales on Both Sides

Ride-hailing showed powerful network effects as all-time riders more than doubled to 3.4 million and have already reached about 3.8 million in early 2026. Registered drivers surged 72% to 450,000 and roughly 490,000 by early 2026, reflecting growing supply and rising liquidity in the marketplace.

Geographic Expansion Broadens Addressable Market

The company now operates in 20 cities that together represent about 80% of Turkey’s GDP, significantly expanding its economic footprint. Ride-hailing was launched in 16 additional cities and delivery started in Istanbul, boosting network density and setting the stage for future monetization.

Multiservice Adoption Lifts ARPU

Marti’s multiservice strategy is gaining traction, with 35% of car-hailing and 82% of motorcycle-hailing users originating from another company service. These multiservice consumers generate 4.4 times the trips and 3.6 times the revenue of single-service users, while many drivers are already flexing between ride-hailing and delivery.

Cost Discipline and AI Support Efficiency

Cost of revenues fell 29% to $15.3 million despite strong volume growth, helped by operational efficiencies, lower depreciation and logistics savings. Management highlighted AI-enabled tools as a contributor to faster and higher-quality software development and product improvements, reinforcing margin gains.

Capital Efficiency and Liquidity Cushion

Marti ended the year with about $8 million of cash and has drawn $13 million on a convertible note facility with another $10 million available. Management argued this liquidity is sufficient to execute near-term plans without immediate fundraising, assuming progress stays on track.

Profitability Still a Work in Progress

Despite clear improvement, the company’s adjusted EBITDA remains negative and requires further gains in pricing, utilization and cost control to reach break-even. The push to turn a $12.1 million loss into a $1 million profit by 2026 is ambitious and leaves little room for missteps.

Monetization Still Concentrated in Few Cities

Monetization remained limited in 2025, with only three cities contributing meaningfully by year-end, leaving much of the footprint underpriced. The revenue growth story now hinges on successfully rolling out monetization across additional cities while maintaining user growth and competitive positioning.

2-Wheeler Fleet Shrinkage a Short-Term Constraint

Marti’s average deployed two-wheeled electric vehicle fleet declined about 29% year over year due to decommissioning older units. This reduction could temporarily constrain capacity in two-wheeler services, although management appears focused on higher-quality utilization over sheer fleet size.

Delivery Business in Early Build-Out Phase

The delivery segment launched late in the fourth quarter and is initially focused on parcel and courier use cases rather than large-scale food or grocery. Management framed delivery as a strategic adjacency with modest near-term revenue impact but potentially valuable incremental use of the existing driver base.

G&A Shows Mixed Signals

Reported general and administrative expense fell 43% to $28.1 million, mainly because of lower share-based compensation. However, G&A excluding stock comp increased 39% to $16.8 million, reflecting higher underlying operating costs required to support the company’s rapid scale.

Take Rate Below Global Benchmarks

Management acknowledged that current take rates sit in the high single to low double digits, well below the roughly 30% global benchmark. Future revenue upside depends on Marti’s ability to raise take rates gradually without losing riders or drivers, making pricing strategy a key lever.

Macro and Regulatory Clouds on the Horizon

The company flagged macro risks such as inflation and fuel-price swings, partially cushioned by government subsidies so far. It also faces an evolving national regulatory framework that could shift competitive dynamics and potentially increase customer acquisition or driver subsidy costs.

Cash Position Leaves Limited Margin for Error

With only about $8 million in cash and reliance on remaining convertible note capacity, Marti is sensitive to any slowdown in growth or monetization. If execution falls short, the company could need to revisit funding options sooner than planned, a key risk for investors watching the path to profitability.

Guidance and Outlook Signal Confidence in Momentum

Management guided to $70 million of revenue and $1 million of positive adjusted EBITDA in 2026, building on the 2025 revenue of $39.2 million and sharply improved margins. The outlook assumes broader city monetization, modest take-rate increases, growing delivery contributions and continued rider and driver growth funded by existing cash and remaining note capacity.

Marti’s earnings call painted a picture of a platform at an inflection point, with rapid growth, stronger margins and rising engagement offset by lingering losses and funding sensitivity. For investors, the story now turns on execution: expanding monetization, nudging take rates higher and maintaining volume growth, all while steering carefully through macro and regulatory risks.

data.title: Pioneer Minerals Advances Idaho Critical Minerals Prospect With Key Drilling Permit Step
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Pioneer Lithium Limited ( (AU:PMM) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Pioneer Minerals Limited is an ASX-listed exploration company focused on critical minerals, with a particular emphasis on tungsten, gallium and precious metals. The company’s key asset is the North Pine Project in Idaho, where it is advancing the Springfield Prospect, a historic mine area being evaluated for multi-commodity development potential within U.S. domestic supply chains.

The release outlines Pioneer’s submission of a Plan of Operations to the U.S. Forest Service for a low-impact drilling program at the Springfield Prospect, marking a major permitting milestone toward drilling in the 2026 field season. The proposed campaign includes up to 15 reverse circulation holes and 20 shallow holes to test high-grade tungsten, gold and newly defined gallium mineralisation, underpinning Springfield’s status as an emerging multi-commodity critical minerals project and supporting future studies on processing, access and potential U.S. funding support.

More about Pioneer Lithium Limited

Pioneer Minerals Limited is an ASX-listed exploration company focused on critical minerals, with a particular emphasis on tungsten, gallium and precious metals. The company’s key asset is the North Pine Project in Idaho, where it is advancing the Springfield Prospect, a historic mine area being evaluated for multi-commodity development potential within U.S. domestic supply chains.

data.title: INFRONEER Clarifies Nikkei Report on Potential Swing Corporation Acquisition
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from INFRONEER Holdings Inc. ( (JP:5076) ).

INFRONEER Holdings Inc. has addressed a Nikkei report claiming the company has decided to acquire Swing Corporation for more than JPY 90 billion, clarifying that the information did not originate from the company. The infrastructure-focused group, led by Representative Executive Officer and President Kazunari Kibe, remains a key player in Japan’s engineering and construction ecosystem.

The company stated it is currently in discussions regarding a possible acquisition of Swing Corporation and plans to submit the matter to its Board of Directors at a meeting scheduled for today. INFRONEER added that, should any decision be made that requires disclosure, it will promptly announce it, signaling to investors and other stakeholders that the deal is still under consideration rather than finalized.

More about INFRONEER Holdings Inc.

INFRONEER Holdings Inc. is a Japan-based company listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market under securities code 5076. The group operates in the infrastructure and related services sector, with its business focused on engineering, construction and maintenance of social and industrial infrastructure in domestic and potentially overseas markets.

data.title: Ausgold drilling unlocks high-grade upside at Katanning Gold Project
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ausgold Limited ( (AU:AUC) ) has provided an update.

Ausgold has reported a series of high-grade assay results from reverse circulation and diamond drilling within the Central Zone of its Katanning Gold Project in Western Australia. The campaign, now 46,717m into a planned 54,000m program, is targeting both resource growth and improved confidence in the early years of planned production.

Recent drilling has delivered standout gold intercepts of up to 41.25g/t, with broad zones of mineralisation intersected beneath current pit designs and outside existing ore reserves. These results point to strong potential to grow reserves, extend mine life and enhance grade continuity, particularly at the Jinkas and White Dam lodes.

Extensional drilling along the White Dam–Jackson trends has also confirmed mineralisation at depth, reinforcing the down-plunge growth potential of the deposit. In parallel, drilling at the nearby Nanicup Bridge target has been completed to support a maiden satellite resource, which could further bolster the project’s production base.

Management highlighted that the consistency of high-grade results from both in-fill and extensional drilling strengthens Katanning’s growth narrative. The company sees a clear opportunity to expand its production profile and optimise the early years of its mine plan as outstanding assay results are received and incorporated into future resource and reserve updates.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:AUC) stock is a Buy with a A$3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ausgold Limited stock, see the AU:AUC Stock Forecast page.

More about Ausgold Limited

Ausgold Limited is an Australian gold exploration and development company focused on its 100%-owned Katanning Gold Project in Western Australia. The project sits within a major mineralised structural corridor and hosts multi-lode gold mineralisation across three key resource zones—Northern, Central and Southern—over a 15km trend within a 3,000km² regional landholding.

YTD Price Performance: -11.82%

Average Trading Volume: 1,636,187

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$531.4M
